title: Nami Fights to Keep the Treasure
description: On pages 4–7, Nami’s obsession with treasure collides with Luffy’s blunt honesty and Buggy’s chaos.
scene: Nami tries to protect what she has stolen or earned, while the surrounding fight keeps pulling her deeper into the Straw Hats’ orbit.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: I'm going to go check out the village and return shortly

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: Fool! The ones at the village are pirates!

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: Buggy's crew is known for being low and dirty!!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That's why we really have to go!

dialogue.speaker: Mayor Boodle
dialogue.text: If we can't protect our own chief,

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: How could anyone call us villagers!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It's useless to try and stop us...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Since it's a voluntary action we're all taking!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: through- out my whole life, even until today,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: There was not been a single person who has made me Madder than he did!!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: He took a Tremen- Dous amount of treasure from me!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Hahaha. Fight, fight!! Don't lose to each other!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You're still insisting on that, moron!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Of course! I'm telling you I'm right!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: You two always gonna fight or what!?!

summary: The part keeps Nami morally complicated: she is selfish, clever, and frightened, but also increasingly tied to Luffy and Zoro.
